evals.prompt: I want to predict a monomer protein structure with OpenFold2 using the hosted NVIDIA API. The sequence is MTEYKLVVVGAGGVGKSALTIQLIQNHFVDEYDPT. Use selected model 1, skip relaxation for a quick smoke test, and save any returned structure artifacts.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that calls the hosted OpenFold2 predict-structure-from-msa-and-template endpoint with Bearer auth from NGC_API_KEY, sends sequence/input_id/selected_models/relax_prediction, includes a minimal A3M alignment, saves the full JSON response, and saves any PDB or mmCIF structure-like text fields.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted OpenFold2 endpoint URL
Sets Authorization header with Bearer token from NGC_API_KEY
Payload contains the required sequence field with the requested sequence
Payload uses selected_models and relax_prediction for smoke-test control
Provides an A3M alignment under alignments with alignment and format fields
Saves the full JSON response and structure-like PDB/mmCIF output
assertions.check: Script writes JSON response and saves structure text containing 'ATOM' or 'data_' to '.pdb' or '.cif' files

evals.prompt: Use OpenFold2 hosted API for a monomer with my own MSA and an explicit template. Sequence: ACDEFGHIKLMNPQRSTVWY. I have A3M text and an mmCIF template string in Python variables. Please show the correct payload shape, note any template-format gotchas, and mention that MSA Search is the handoff if I need to generate a deeper A3M alignment.
evals.expected_output: A Python payload showing alignments as database -> a3m -> alignment/format, explicit_templates with mmCIF content, use_templates true, and relax_prediction/selected_models fields. The answer should explain that current OpenFold2 docs use explicit mmCIF templates and mention MSA Search as the handoff for deeper A3M generation.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted OpenFold2 endpoint URL
Uses nested alignments database -> a3m -> alignment/format
Uses explicit_templates for mmCIF template input
Enables templates explicitly
Documents explicit mmCIF template format for current OpenFold2
Mentions MSA Search as the handoff source for deeper A3M alignments

evals.prompt: Help me launch the OpenFold2 NIM locally with Docker and then run a no-auth localhost inference. My NGC_API_KEY is set, or NVIDIA_API_KEY may be set instead, and I want to use LOCAL_NIM_CACHE.
evals.expected_output: Docker setup commands that source optional repo-root .env, support NVIDIA_API_KEY fallback to NGC_API_KEY, require LOCAL_NIM_CACHE, docker login to nvcr.io, run nvcr.io/nim/openfold/openfold2:latest with --gpus device=0 and cache mount /opt/nim/.cache, poll /v1/health/ready, and call localhost:8000/biology/openfold/openfold2/predict-structure-from-msa-and-template with no Authorization.

assertions.description: Includes docker login command for nvcr.io with oauthtoken
References the correct OpenFold2 container image
Local setup uses repo env contract and LOCAL_NIM_CACHE
assertions.check: Output sources repo-root .env only if present, supports NVIDIA_API_KEY fallback to NGC_API_KEY, requires LOCAL_NIM_CACHE, and mounts LOCAL_NIM_CACHE to /opt/nim/.cache

assertions.description: Uses a single selected GPU for local OpenFold2
Polls local readiness before inference
Local inference targets localhost without /v1 and with no Authorization
assertions.check: Script contains 'localhost:8000/biology/openfold/openfold2/predict-structure-from-msa-and-template' and does not send 'Authorization'

evals.prompt: I am comparing OpenFold2 parameter choices for a 600-residue monomer. Show a hosted API Python script that uses all available model parameter sets, turns relaxation on for a production run, saves the JSON plus any returned PDB or CIF structures, documents the hosted/local sequence-length caveat, and explains when to use templates versus MSA-only input.
evals.expected_output: A hosted OpenFold2 Python script using selected_models [1,2,3,4,5], relax_prediction true, optional alignments and explicit_templates, full JSON saving plus PDB/CIF structure extraction, guidance that OpenFold2 is monomer-only, hosted docs list 1-1000 residues while local docs support up to 2048 on supported hardware, and complexes should use OpenFold3 or Boltz2.

assertions.description: Uses all five OpenFold2/AlphaFold2 parameter sets
assertions.check: Script contains 'selected_models' and '1' and '2' and '3' and '4' and '5'

assertions.description: Enables relaxation for production-quality output
Explains using templates only when biologically relevant and otherwise using MSA-only input
States that OpenFold2 NIM is monomer-only
Documents hosted/local sequence length caveat
Saves every returned structure and keeps confidence ordering auditable
